description: Two heterogeneous LMs in one joint generative program: a grammar-constrained VLM GF (trend judgment over a rendered scatter) whose traced choice conditions a coder GF that proposes probabilistic programs, scored by exact evidence. The VLM is an advisory prior; the reference ladder is the evidence's own verdict on the data.

config.prompt_revision: 2026-07-11: quadratic DSL instruction strengthened with a worked obs-site example after the first freeze's forced arm emitted a collinear curvature*x model; this artifact is the FIRST run under the revised prompt (genmlx-8dfk).
config.jitter_note: Large-MoE forwards are kernel-level nondeterministic (genmlx-cnhi); transcripts are one-shot frozen fixtures, scores re-derived from frozen :code are deterministic.

rows.code:
(fn [trace]
  (let [intercept (trace :intercept (dist/gaussian 0 20))
        slope (trace :slope (dist/gaussian 0 20))]
    (trace :y0 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 0)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y1 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 1)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y2 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 2)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y3 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 3)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y4 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 4)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y5 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 5)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y6 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 6)) intercept) 1))
    (trace :y7 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 7)) intercept) 1))))

(fn [trace]
  (let [intercept (trace :intercept (dist/gaussian 0 20))
        slope (trace :slope (dist/gaussian 0 20))
        curvature (trace :curvature (dist/gaussian 0 20))]
    (trace :y0 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 0)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 0)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y1 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 1)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 1)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y2 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 4)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 2)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y3 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 9)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 3)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y4 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 16)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 4)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y5 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 25)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 5)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y6 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 36)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 6)) intercept)) 1))
    (trace :y7 (dist/gaussian (mx/add (mx/multiply curvature (mx/scalar 49)) (mx/add (mx/multiply slope (mx/scalar 7)) intercept)) 1))))

(fn [trace]
  (let [mu (trace :mu (dist/gaussian 0 20))]
    (trace :y0 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y1 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y2 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y3 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y4 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y5 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y6 (dist/gaussian mu 1))
    (trace :y7 (dist/gaussian mu 1))))
